title: Audio: Two Pointer — the family map

cues.label: The O(n²) pair search
cues.headline: A nested loop over every pair
cues.narration: Finding a pair that sums to a target by checking all pairs is quadratic work.

cues.label: Sorted makes a comparison decisive
cues.headline: One comparison kills a side
cues.narration: On a sorted row, a too-big sum means the largest value is hopeless — drop the whole right side.

cues.label: Converging branch
cues.headline: Fingers walk toward each other
cues.narration: Left starts low, right starts high; each comparison advances one of them inward.

cues.label: Read / write branch
cues.headline: Same direction, two jobs
cues.narration: Read scans every element; write marks the boundary of the compacted answer built in place.

cues.label: Fast / slow branch
cues.headline: One path, two speeds
cues.narration: A speed gap that shrinks by one each tick forces a collision, exposing cycles and midpoints.

cues.label: When it fails
cues.headline: No structure, no license
cues.narration: Unsorted data breaks converging pointers; force the pattern and you get a fast wrong answer.

cues.label: Recognition & window kinship
cues.headline: Fingerprints and cousins
cues.narration: A sliding window is a same-direction two-pointer; recognize on structure, not surface words.
